blocks: Before the semester begins, before the syllabus is printed, before any student opens the assignment prompt — something h
Chapter 8 — Assessment in the Age of Cla — This is not a new kind of academic dishonesty. It is a new condition of the assignment.

S02.meta: // SLIDE 02 — WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

data.title: WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO.
blocks: · Identify which parts of your current assessments are vulnerable to AI substitution and which parts are not
· Apply five assessment design patterns that produce evidence of student thinking
· Redesign a vulnerable take-home assessment into a process-based design
· Write an AI-use disclosure requirement that is teachable, not punitive
· Distinguish between assessment redesign and surveillance, and understand why the distinction matters
data.section: WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

data.title: CORE CONCEPTS
blocks: Assessment validity
Authentic assessment
Process evidence
AI-use disclosure
Cognitive labor

data.title: THE VULNERABILITY MAP.
blocks: · Take-home essays on general or well-documented topics
· Research summaries
· Multiple-choice and short-answer tests given as take-home or untimed work
· Discussion board posts on abstract topics
· Lab or case reports with standard templates

data.title: FIVE ASSESSMENT DESIGN PATTERNS.
blocks: · A pre-writing brainstorm or outline showing the student's thinking before drafting
· Two or more drafts with revision notes explaining what the student changed and why
A brief reflection What was hard? What did you learn from revising? What would you change if you had more time?

data.title: REDESIGNING A VULNERABLE ASSESSMENT: A WORKED EXAMPLE
blocks: Process requirement Students submit an annotated bibliography (with brief notes on each source in their own words) one week before the essay
Transfer element The essay prompt is refined to include a case the teacher introduces the week before the due date — a specific recent ev

· What can Claude produce that would pass this assessment without the student understanding the material? Be honest. This
· What design feature creates evidence of student thinking? If the answer is "the student promised not to use AI," that is

data.title: COMMON MISTAKES
blocks: Reacting to AI with prohibition only.
Ignoring the equity dimension of surveillance.
Redesigning so much that students stop learning from assessment.
Treating all AI use as equivalent.
Waiting for institutional policy before acting.
